title: baccharis tola var. incarum
intro: Baccharis tola var. incarum, also known as Incarnate dwarf shrub, is a plant species within the daisy family that is native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The medicinal properties of Baccharis tola var. incarum include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Baccharis tola var. incarum is traditionally prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with its leaves and stems being used for medicinal purposes.
